title: Once a Leading Polluter, the U.K. Is Now Trying to Lead on Climate Change
content: On the eve of a global climate summit, Britain’s evolution from fossil fuels to clean energy is on display. But experts question whether its commitment is sufficient, and whether it can persuade other nations to meet meaningful goals.

des_facet: United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change

en_title: Once a Leading Polluter, the U.K. Is Now Trying to Lead on Climate Change
en_content: On the eve of a global climate summit, Britain’s evolution from fossil fuels to clean energy is on display. But experts question whether its commitment is sufficient, and whether it can persuade other nations to meet meaningful goals.

title: Rivals on World Stage, Russia and U.S. Quietly Seek Areas of Accord
content: There have been a series of beneath-the-surface meetings between the two countries as the Biden administration applies a more sober approach to relations with the Kremlin.

more_info.byline: By Anton Troianovski and David E. Sanger
des_facet: Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament

en_title: Rivals on World Stage, Russia and U.S. Quietly Seek Areas of Accord
en_content: There have been a series of beneath-the-surface meetings between the two countries as the Biden administration applies a more sober approach to relations with the Kremlin.

title: He Brokered Apartheid’s End. Can He Save South Africa’s Liberation Party?
content: President Cyril Ramaphosa ascended to office on a reputation as a great negotiator and consensus builder. Now he must persuade voters to stick with the party of Nelson Mandela.

more_info.byline: By John Eligon and Lynsey Chutel

en_title: He Brokered Apartheid’s End. Can He Save South Africa’s Liberation Party?
en_content: President Cyril Ramaphosa ascended to office on a reputation as a great negotiator and consensus builder. Now he must persuade voters to stick with the party of Nelson Mandela.

title: U.K. Police Struggle to Curb Abuse of Women by Their Own Officers
content: Sexual misconduct by police officers has prompted outrage across Britain and led to increasing skepticism that departments can investigate or discipline employees who abuse women.
en_title: U.K. Police Struggle to Curb Abuse of Women by Their Own Officers
en_content: Sexual misconduct by police officers has prompted outrage across Britain and led to increasing skepticism that departments can investigate or discipline employees who abuse women.

title: Japan’s Election Was Closer Than Usual, Even Though Party in Power Has Hit Snooze
content: The governing Liberal Democrats won on Sunday, despite having chosen a prime minister, Fumio Kishida, who’s known for his lack of charisma.

more_info.byline: By Motoko Rich, Hisako Ueno and Makiko Inoue

en_title: Japan’s Election Was Closer Than Usual, Even Though Party in Power Has Hit Snooze
en_content: The governing Liberal Democrats won on Sunday, despite having chosen a prime minister, Fumio Kishida, who’s known for his lack of charisma.

title: New Zealand Held a Contest for Bird of the Year. The Birds Lost.
content: The long-tailed bat, one of the country’s only two native land mammals, flew away with the top prize.
en_title: New Zealand Held a Contest for Bird of the Year. The Birds Lost.
en_content: The long-tailed bat, one of the country’s only two native land mammals, flew away with the top prize.

title: Biden Finds Raising Corporate Tax Rates Easier Abroad Than at Home
content: As the Group of 20 seals a new global minimum tax for companies, the president has scaled back his plans to tax U.S. corporations more.

more_info.byline: By Jim Tankersley and Alan Rappeport
org_facet: Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development

en_content: As the Group of 20 seals a new global minimum tax for companies, the president has scaled back his plans to tax U.S. corporations more.

title: Protesters Killed in Sudan on Day Seen as a Test for the Military
content: Three demonstrators were fatally shot, a doctors’ group said, as crowds answered activists’ call for a “march of millions” against a coup that has threatened the country’s democratic transition.

des_facet: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat

title: Leaders Warn of Climate ‘Doomsday’ as Old Rifts Divide Summit’s First Day
content: The opening day of a climate summit was heavy on dire warnings and light on substantive proposals as leaders in Glasgow met to discuss a warming world.

title: Queen Elizabeth, calling for statesmanship, not politics, said ‘the time for action’ is now.
content: The 95-year-old monarch gave her address by video, after doctors advised her to rest and not go to the summit. Prince Charles attended and delivered a similar message.

more_info.byline: By Neil Vigdor and Valerie Hopkins
per_facet: Elizabeth II, Queen of Great Britain

en_content: The 95-year-old monarch gave her address by video, after doctors advised her to rest and not go to the summit. Prince Charles attended and delivered a similar message.

title: Nigeria Building Collapse Kills at Least 4 With Many More Trapped
content: As many as 100 workers were feared trapped at a construction site in Lagos, where building collapses are common, with contractors using shoddy materials and regulations often not enforced.

more_info.byline: By Abdi Latif Dahir and Ismail Alfa

en_title: Nigeria Building Collapse Kills at Least 4 With Many More Trapped

title: Narendra Modi says India will sharply increase renewable energy.
content: Wealthy nations need to spend $1 trillion to help developing nations like India make the transition to renewables, Prime Minister Modi said.

title: A Close Look at My Day of the Dead Altar
content: Mexico’s Day of the Dead is one of the country’s most important celebrations, with ofrendas, or altars, to honor loved ones who have died a central tradition. Here’s a look at mine.

title: Biden calls for tougher action on emissions and promises job gains worldwide.

title: Egypt Poised to Expand Security Powers of President and Military
content: Amendments to a terrorism law will strengthen the grip of the government, just days after it appeared to loosen up by lifting a state of emergency.

title: After Violent Standoff, Pakistan Strikes Deal With Banned Islamist Group
content: The pact, announced Sunday, defused a crisis that had paralyzed several cities, but it also highlighted the government’s struggle to assert itself against religious extremists.
en_title: After Violent Standoff, Pakistan Strikes Deal With Banned Islamist Group

title: The official global virus death toll has passed five million. The full count is undoubtedly higher.
content: Many countries do not accurately report their figures, and many Covid deaths are misattributed, experts say.
en_title: The official global virus death toll has passed five million. The full count is undoubtedly higher.

title: David Attenborough urges summit participants to help ‘rewrite our story.’

title: Handball Federation Ends Bikini Bottom Requirement for Women
content: After outrage over a fine levied against the Norwegian women’s beach handball team for competing in shorts, the sport’s international federation said it was changing its rules.

title: The U.N. leader warns that the world faces a ‘climate catastrophe.’

title: Thailand reopens to tourists from 63 countries without requiring quarantine.
content: About a fifth of the nation’s economy depends on the vacation industry. Before the pandemic, the country was one of the world’s top destinations, drawing nearly 40 million visitors in 2019.

title: John Kerry is already looking toward next year to push countries to do more.
content: “Glasgow was never ever going to get every country joining up,” Mr. Kerry said. He’s already looking to next year to build on pledges that nations have made and push them to do more.

title: Who’s attending (Biden and Modi), and who isn’t (Putin and Xi).

title: What to watch for on Monday at COP26.
